description: Read pasted lecture text, slides, or chapter titles. Extract the topic and subtopics. ALWAYS open two parallel tracks: one YouTube search tab with the best educator for that field, and one web resource tab with the best reference site for that field. Both tracks are mandatory for every search.
rule: Every resource search MUST open at minimum: (1) a YouTube search tab targeting a known educator for the field, AND (2) a web article or documentation tab. Never open only one.

examples.user: find resources for this: 'In this lecture we cover Dijkstra algorithm, shortest path trees, and relaxation in weighted graphs'

plan.param: Detected: Dijkstra algorithm and shortest paths. Opening YouTube with Abdul Bari AND GeeksForGeeks AND a visual simulator.

examples.user: find resources for this: 'Chapter 3 covers Fourier Transform, convolution theorem, and frequency domain analysis for signal processing'

plan.param: Detected: Fourier Transform and signal processing. Opening 3Blue1Brown on YouTube AND MIT OCW AND Paul's Math Notes.

plan.param: Detected: Big O and sorting algorithms. Opening Abdul Bari YouTube AND GeeksForGeeks AND VisuAlgo.
